channel.title: Ecclesiology After à Brakel
channel.description: A personal audio feed — reflections on the church from Wilhelmus à Brakel's "The Reasonable Service of God." Read by Jason Ruis.
channel.itunes:author: Jason Ruis
channel.itunes:summary: Personal audio reflections on Reformed ecclesiology, working through à Brakel's The Reasonable Service of God. Not a public podcast — just for personal listening.

item.title: 22 — Letter 16: The Keys and Your Soul
item.description: Series 3 finale. à Brakel's last letter to a young parishioner — the two keys (preaching and discipline) from the member's perspective, the four steps of discipline, restoration when there is repentance, and the accounting every member will give. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 29.

item.title: 21 — Letter 15: Shepherds and Servants
item.description: à Brakel writes to a church member about pastors, elders, and deacons — what each office does and how members should receive them. The pastoral office's six duties, the elders as watchmen, the diaconate as a spiritual office. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 28.

item.title: 20 — Letter 14: The Man in the Pulpit
item.description: à Brakel writes to a church member about how to receive your pastor as Christ's ambassador. The calling of the minister, the meaning of ordination, and why the way you listen to sermons determines whether the Word does its work in your soul. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 27.

item.title: 19 — Letter 13: Who Is Your King?
item.description: à Brakel writes to a church member about church government from the member's perspective. No pope, no bishop over other shepherds, no celebrity pastor. Christ alone governs through equals. The assemblies are a chain of counsel, not command. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 27.

item.title: 18 — Letter 12: The Family You've Been Given
item.description: à Brakel writes to a church member about the communion of saints — the six acts of communal life (worshiping, keeping peace, showing love, giving and following examples, helping one another grow, rendering bodily help). "Most of us attend church. Few of us exercise communion. Those are not the same thing." From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 26.

item.title: 17 — Letter 11: Christ Is Yours
item.description: à Brakel writes to a church member about communion with Christ — ownership, union, and communion with the Person. The six ways to exercise communion: contemplating, loving, conversing, depending, asking counsel, using His goods. "All mine is thine, and thine is mine." From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 26.

item.title: 16 — Letter 10: When You Want to Walk Away
item.description: à Brakel writes to a church member about staying when the church disappoints. The separatist temptation for laypeople, the halfway separation of attending but never committing, and why you cannot skip the Lord's Supper even when the church is corrupt. "The church is irreformable, inconvertible, hopeless — and you stay anyway." From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 25.

item.title: 15 — Letter 09: The Church Is Not Optional
item.description: Series 3 premiere. à Brakel begins writing letters to a young parishioner — a church member, not a pastor. The church is not a product you consume. She is Christ's body and bride, bought with His blood. The church you have been given is the church Christ gave you. Love her, not because she is lovely, but because He is. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapters 25-26.

item.title: 14 — Letter 08: The Keys and the Accounting
item.description: Series 2 finale. à Brakel's last letter to a young pastor — the two keys (preaching the Word and the Christian ban), the four steps of discipline, the magistrate's role concerning but not in the church, and the accounting every overseer will give for the souls entrusted to him. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 29.

item.title: 13 — Letter 07: The Work and the Men Beside You
item.description: à Brakel's job description for a young pastor — the seven qualifications and six duties of the pastoral office (praying, preaching, catechizing, visiting, sacraments, discipline), plus the work of ruling elders and the diaconate. "There is no more abominable creature under the sun than a minister who has himself in view." From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 28.

item.title: 12 — Letter 06: You Are Sent
item.description: à Brakel writes to a young pastor about the calling of the minister — the inward calling (knowledge of the office, knowledge of your own ability, love for Christ and souls, denial of all things, desire for the work) and the outward calling (from the church, not the magistrate). Self-examination questions, and the warning: "If you are not called, go be a cobbler." From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 27.

item.title: 11 — Letter 05: No King but Christ
item.description: à Brakel writes to a young pastor about church government — against the papacy, against episcopal hierarchy, against the celebrity pastor. No pope, no bishop over other shepherds. Christ alone governs through equals, sent through His church. The assemblies are a chain of counsel, not a chain of command. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 27.

item.title: 10 — Letter 04: The Flock Together
item.description: à Brakel writes to a young pastor about the communion of saints — the six acts of communal life (worshiping together, keeping peace, showing love, giving and following examples, helping one another grow spiritually, rendering bodily help), the church as refreshment, and no community of goods. "Most of us attend church. Few of us exercise communion. Those are not the same thing." From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 26.

item.title: 09 — Letter 03: Your Own Soul First
item.description: à Brakel writes to a young pastor about communion with Christ — ownership, union, and communion with the Person (not just the goods). The six ways to exercise communion: contemplating, loving, conversing, depending, asking counsel, using His goods. The warning against ministering from emptiness. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 26.

item.title: 08 — Letter 02: Stay When You Want to Run
item.description: à Brakel writes to a young pastor about staying in the church when she disappoints — the separatist impulse, the "purer church" delusion, the halfway separation of attending but never committing, and why you cannot skip the Lord's Supper even when the church is corrupt. "The church is irreformable, inconvertible, hopeless — and you stay anyway." From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 25.

item.title: 07 — Letter 01: The Church You've Been Given
item.description: Series 2 premiere. à Brakel begins writing letters to a young pastor he is mentoring. The church is not an optional add-on to following Jesus — she is Christ's body and bride, bought with His blood. The church you have been given is the church Christ gave you. Love her, not because she is lovely, but because He is. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapters 25-26.

item.title: 06 — The Keys and the Ban
item.description: à Brakel on the ecclesiastical power and the keys of the kingdom of heaven — consistories, classes, synods; the first key (preaching the Word); the second key (the Christian ban and church discipline); church and magistrates (Belgic Confession Art. 36); and the exhortation to overseers to use the keys faithfully. The finale of the ecclesiology series. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 29.

item.title: 05 — Feed My Sheep: The Job Description
item.description: à Brakel's job description for church officers — the qualifications and six duties of pastors (praying, preaching, catechizing, visiting, sacraments, discipline), the work of ruling elders, the diaconate, and the accounting every officer will give. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 28.

item.title: 04 — No Throne in the Church but Christ's
item.description: à Brakel against the papacy and episcopal hierarchy — no visible head, no bishop over other shepherds, and the calling of ministers inward and outward, from the church not from magistrates. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 27.

item.title: 03 — All Mine Is Thine, and Thine Is Mine
item.description: à Brakel on the communion of believers with Christ and with one another — ownership, union, and communion with Christ's Person and His goods, and the communion of the militant and triumphant church. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 26.

item.title: 02 — You Do Not Get to Leave
item.description: à Brakel's case against forsaking the church — why you must never leave to build a purer one, and why you must not abstain from the Lord's Supper even when the church is corrupt. From "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 25, sections VIII–XXX.

item.title: 01 — There Is No Christ Without His Church
item.description: à Brakel's case for why every Christian must join the church — five proofs and five motives, from "The Reasonable Service of God," Volume 2, Chapter 25.
